data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-22T23:02 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-0rlgv [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-2319d: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-mql5q: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-p1jrb: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-t1tk5: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2

content.thinking: All children are marked with ✓ (completed). This patrol cycle is already done. I should check `bd mol current` to see if there's a next step, or squash this wisp and create a new patrol.

content.text: All steps complete. Squashing this wisp and starting a new patrol cycle.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary "Patrol complete: all steps already closed from previous session"

content.thinking: Dolt database crash - nil pointer dereference. This is a known issue with the embedded Dolt store. Let me try a different approach - just create a new patrol directly since the old one's steps are all complete.

content.text: Dolt storage crash on squash. Skipping squash, creating fresh patrol directly.
